pcs.name: Spyke Ateles
pcs.description: Wrench jockey with a solid reputation and a union card. Member of one of Argent's new-wave labor unions — less than ten years old, which means he helped build the thing he fights for. Understands labor politics from the inside, not the theory. His older brother is a Loyalist. His father was killed in an incident blamed on a Loyalist Guardsman — evidence was circumstantial, and the case went nowhere.

pcs.name: Brother Jerome
pcs.description: Ivory tower academic stepping into a world that doesn't match the theory. True believer in the Motherboard faith — and ambitious. Right now the faith and the ambition point in the same direction. All his grimoires are loaded on a future-tech tablet that doubles as a spellcasting prop — the faith and the performance are already blurred for him. He wants to matter: within the faith, within the world, to the Motherboard herself.

pcs.name: Niff Nimble-Ray
pcs.ikonis: Shocking Touch — shock stick, Very Close range
augments: Nanite Control Module (spine — creates wings, fuels techno-magic, enables Wildshape)
pcs.description: Self-proclaimed techno-naturalist. Fairies in Echo Vale are smaller than average with bug-like features — Niff's Nanite Control Module handles the rest: wings, techno-magic, and the ability to Wildshape into Remnant forms. Spent years observing Remnants by shifting into them before the Fury made that dangerous. Tends to forget people's names and faces. Her attention genuinely lives somewhere else.

npcs.name: BR-31489KJ8945 ("BR")
npcs.status: In party custody. Condition suppressed approximately 12 hours from end of Session 2.
npcs.description: A worker-class Remnant in party custody, fighting something from the inside. Cycles through states — calm, warning, communicative, and back again. During communicative phases, tries to spell words in Kohd. Carries a non-standard modification that the party doesn't fully understand. Removing it would be fatal.
npcs.player_notes: BR is actively resisting whatever has been done to it. The cycling is the resistance, not the infection.

npcs.name: Brem Daltock
npcs.description: Factory owner and Loyalist. Genuine warmth and pure self-interest, sometimes in the same breath — he never pretends or postures. Augmented: cybernetic right eyeball and a multipurpose left-arm prosthetic. Former Guild Administrator who helped found three unions and wrote two constitutions before turning Loyalist about four years ago. Some Unionists still flinch calling him an enemy.
npcs.player_notes: He's not pretending. The warmth is real. So is everything else.

npcs.name: Priv Maelor Rhodek
npcs.description: The legitimate authority in Argent. Female elf, Orderborne. Charismatic and authoritative — not cruel, but clearly operating under pressure. Publicly: maintain order, suppress the Fury, hold the city together. The Loyalists' increasing violence has her fingerprints on it, and whatever is driving that urgency, it's real.
npcs.player_notes: Met the party at Mac's shop — a clandestine meeting, not an accident. She gave them the job.

npcs.name: Cooper Finn
npcs.description: Orc Unionist. Spyke sponsored his official union membership. Knew Brem personally during the union founding days — Brem gave him the framework he uses to stand where he stands now. He can't hate Brem cleanly. He remembers who Brem was before the betrayal, and that version was genuine. Measured. Tired. Still believes. Doesn't perform it anymore.
npcs.player_notes: Measured. Tired. Still believes. Doesn't perform it anymore.

npcs.name: Lysander
npcs.description: Dwarf. Loyalist Guard 2IC at the factory. Projects indifference — short answers, minimum information, hand already on his Ikonis. Not the type to volunteer more than he has to. But he has his own concerns about where things are heading, and occasionally that shows.
npcs.player_notes: He flagged his own commanding officer. That means something.

npcs.name: Kael Morst
npcs.role: Loyalist Guard Captain at the factory
npcs.description: Loyalist Guard Captain. Rigid, humorless, and looking for a reason to escalate. There's something off about him — not corrupt exactly, but not entirely operating within his authority either. His own second-in-command said so.
npcs.player_notes: He's already done the moral math on what he's willing to do.

npcs.name: Mac the Scrap Jack
npcs.description: Wanderborne Faun. Morbidly obese, unpleasant, impossible accent. Runs an illegal scrap operation and fancies himself a fixer. Perpetually in over his head, perpetually looking for leverage on someone, perpetually getting outmaneuvered. Connects people who need things with people who have them — as long as neither party looks too closely at the arrangement.
npcs.player_notes: He doesn't know yet. He will. The question is when and how he moves.

npcs.name: Tuk
npcs.description: Dark-haired, long arms. Almost a caricature of a person. Was working as a planted scab — paid to spread false information blaming Unionists for trouble at the picket line. He's been made. The unions know. His situation is not good.
npcs.player_notes: Tuk is burned. He may turn up again in worse shape.
